data.title: S&P 500 kicks off third year of bull run by notching its longest weekly gain of 2024
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) on Friday added 0.85% for the week to end at 5,864.67 points, posting gains in three out of five sessions. Its accompanying SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) climbed 0.86% for the week.

The benchmark index kicked off the first week of the third year of its current bull run by notching its 46th and 47th record close of 2024. Meanwhile, it was a historic week for the venerable Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI). The blue-chip gauge surpassed the 43,000 points mark for the first time ever, while posting its 37th through 40th record close of the year.

The S&P 500 (SP500) also achieved a six-week win streak, its longest such positive run in 2024. In fact, it was the average's longest weekly win streak since the start of December last year.

Wall Street's advance was driven by a combination of strong earnings, a continued rotation into all corners of the market, a robust retail sales report and increased hopes that the Federal Reserve would be able to deliver a soft landing. Meanwhile, the technology sector went through a roller-coaster courtesy of chip stocks.

Dutch computer chip equipment supplier ASML (ASML) on Tuesday sent shivers through chip stocks after missing quarterly bookings expectations and warning of a "more gradual than previously expected" recovery in market segments that were not related to artificial intelligence (AI). There was also news of U.S. officials mulling further export caps on advanced AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) and other firms.

However, just two days later, sentiment in the chip space sharply reversed course after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) - the world's largest contract chipmaker - announced a blowout quarterly report and reinvigorated the AI trade.

The S&P 500 Financials sector extended its gains from last week, after Morgan Stanley's (MS) earnings showed a rebound in investment banking and dealmaking activity in the quarter, echoing the performance of peers JPMorgan (JPM) and Goldman Sachs (GS).

In other earnings-related moves this week, the Health Care sector was in focus after results from some high-profile names. The managed care space was beset by medical cost concerns following updates from UnitedHealth (UNH) and Elevance Health (ELV). Meanwhile, drugmaker Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) beat top- and bottom-line consensus.

The earnings season becomes a deluge next week, with some of the world's biggest and most-well known firms announcing their financials, including electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA).

This week was also proof of how breadth has improved, shifting out from megacaps and the technology sector to other areas. The Russell 2000 (RTY) index - the barometer for small-caps - climbed +1.91% for the week, outperforming all three major Wall Street indexes.

Additionally, the S&P 500 equal weight index climbed +1.10%, outperforming the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500). The equal weight index includes the same constituents as the capitalization weighted S&P 500, but each company in the equal weight gauge is allocated a fixed weight, meaning that the likes of Nvidia (NVDA) carry the same heft as Dollar Tree (DLTR).

Turning to the weekly performance of the S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, nine of the 11 ended in the green. Utilities and Real Estate topped the winners with gains of more than 3% each. Energy and Health Care were the two losers. See below a breakdown of the performance of the sectors as well as their accompanying SPDR Select Sector ETFs from October 11 close to October 18 close:

#1: Utilities +3.42%, and the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLU) +3.41%.

#2: Real Estate +3.00%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLRE) +3.02%.

#3: Financials +2.43%, and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLF) +2.39%.

#4: Materials +1.92%, and the Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLB) +1.81%.

#5: Consumer Discretionary +1.22%, and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY) +1.49%.

#6: Information Technology +0.81%, and the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLK) +0.04%.

#7: Consumer Staples +0.62%, and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLP) +0.73%.

#8: Industrials +0.60%, and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLI) +0.61%.

#9: Communication Services +0.35%, and the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) +0.88%.

#10: Health Care -0.57%, and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLV) -0.49%.

#11: Energy -2.64%, and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE) -2.66%.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

For investors looking into the future of what's happening, take a look at the Seeking Alpha Catalyst Watch to see next week's breakdown of actionable events that stand out.

More on the markets

  • Is The Market's Melt-Up Due To FOMO?
  • Subsets And Sensibility
  • S&P, Dow both notch new record closes as Wall Street posts six-week win streak
  • Solid labor market fuels real GDP past pre-Covid, pre-Biden admin. growth views: report

data.title: U.S. natural gas wraps worst week since February on mild weather outlook
data.description:
U.S. natural gas futures ended the day and the week down sharply as near-term temperature forecasts point to weak demand while production edges up.

Temperatures are set to rise starting this weekend and by next week will be pushing back into the 70s or higher in many areas from the Great Plains to the Gulf of Mexico to the US East Coast, the National Weather Service said Friday.

The U.S. Climate Prediction Center said above normal temperatures will dominate the entire U.S., except the Pacific Northwest, during October 23-31, and forecaster Commodity Weather Group said weather-driven energy demand may drop to record low levels in the next two weeks.

"Producers appear to be holding back some as prices have stagnated with increasingly warm expectations for winter," Gelber & Associates wrote. "With that said, optionality for producers is immensely valuable in the wintertime to meet sudden demand increases – and, as last January shows, that remains largely true even if forecasts call for overall warm temperatures."

Front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for November delivery settled -3.8% on Friday and 14.2% lower for the week at $2.258/MMBtu, its largest one week percentage decline in eight months.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Gas-focused stocks fell broadly this week, including Antero Resources (AR) -6.4%, Range Resources (RRC) -4.3%, Comstock Resources (CRK) -3.6%, Coterra Energy (CTRA) -2.8%, EQT (EQT) -1.7%, CNX Resources (CNX) -1.1%.

The Matterhorn Express pipeline began pumping natural gas last month from the Permian Basin to the Gulf Coast for export, and energy executives are saying the pipeline is easing pressure on prices, clearing up bottlenecks and boosting U.S. production, Reuters reported Friday.

Earlier this year, Permian producers saw much of the gas produced trapped in the basin, forcing companies to pay for it to be taken away, but with Matterhorn now moving gas, producers are seeing relief, according to the report.

The pipeline stretches 580 miles, and is the first natural gas line to be built in the Permian in the last three years; it has capacity to move 2.5B cf/day of gas, adding 14% in new regional capacity as it ramps up this year.

data.title: Village Farms International receives Nasdaq non-compliance notification
data.description:
• Village Farms International (NASDAQ:VFF) on Friday said it has received notification from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement, as the bid price for the company’s common shares closed below $1.00 from September 6, 2024 to October 17, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spirit Airlines gets cash infusion by maxing out credit facility, shares soar 50% after hours
data.description:
• Shares of Spirit Airlines (NYSE:SAVE) were launched more than 50% higher in Friday’s after-hours trading after the carrier disclosed in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission that it has maxed out its borrowing on a senior secured revolving credit facility.
  • The company has borrowed the entire available amount of $300M under a revolving credit facility which will mature at the end of September 2026. The company added that it will end 2024 with over $1B in liquidity, including unrestricted cash and cash equivalents, assuming that Spirit (SAVE) is able to close on initiatives currently in place.
  • This includes an agreement with its credit card processor to modify the existing Card Processing Agreement to extend to December 23, 2024, from October 21, 2024, and the early maturity date from December 31, 2024, to March 3, 2025.
  • The news, and its likely impact to delay a bankruptcy filing from the struggling carrier, drove shares of JetBlue (JBLU) 6% lower and Frontier Group (ULCC) lower by 4%.

data.title: Freight recovery could still be two years away
data.description:
Old Dominion Freight Lines (NASDAQ:ODFL) closed lower for a second straight day after a downgrade at BMO Capital Markets raised concerns about a recovery in the freight markets. The stock is now rated at Market Perform from Outperform with a new price target of $210. Despite the downgrade, BMO’s Fadi Chamoun still sees ODFL as the best-in-class for less-than-truckload (“LTL”) and recommends investors maintain their position.

Industrial demand – which accounts for two-thirds of LTL traffic – remains sluggish, Chamoun says, and the lag between lower rates and a re-acceleration in demand will likely be pushed out further despite a loosening of monetary policy.

The timing of the downgrade is anticipation of another round of negative EPS revisions with FY25 EPS revisions now placing the current estimate ~15% below BMO’s initial forecast.

“We see limited upside for multiples with valuation sitting at record levels both on an absolute basis and relative to the broader markets,” Chamoun says, adding that he would reconsider his neutral rating on a pullback in valuation or greater clarity into a cyclical recovery.

The downgrade comes just four months after Chamoun initiated Old Dominion (ODFL) with an Outperform rating. At that time, LTL fundamentals for companies like ODFL were improving following the closure of Yellow Corp and the tailwinds from consolidation within the industry.

But the recession within the freight industry continues and has eased only moderately despite lower fuel costs and a slight decline in interest rates.

"Ever since COVID-19, consumer habits, supply chains and other factors were radically changed and have not yet returned to pre-pandemic levels,” Shannon Newton, president of the Arkansas Trucking Association said to Talk Business & Politics. The combination of the East Coast port strike and hurricanes impacting a large part of the eastern seaboard will help inflate trucking rates, but rates remain at a 9-year low. Industry experts believe it will take another two years for the market to get back to equilibrium.

Companies like XPO (XPO) were able to see an increase in profits this year despite a soft market for freight, as increased tonnage in LTL and effective cost management compensated for lower rates.

“Our strong results demonstrate the disciplined progress we’re making with the initiatives we put in place,” said XPO CEO Mario Harik, as cost efficiencies, including more in-sourcing, drove up operating income significantly in Q2.

“Freight rates have largely stabilized, but at unsustainable levels,” said Brad Stewart, Treasurer at Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX) during the company’s recent earnings call, with CFO Andrew Hess adding, “we believe we have positioned [Knight-Swift] to endure a difficult market and to be prepared to rapidly improve margins and cash flow when we begin to experience an inflection in the market.”

data.title: Gold closes above $2,700 for first time as geopolitical tensions simmer
data.description:
Gold settled above $2,700/oz for the first time on Friday, extending a rally driven by uncertainty over the Middle East conflict and the tight U.S. presidential election race, as well as expectations of further central bank interest rate reductions, that have sparked a flight to safety among investors.

Pledges from Israel and its enemies Hamas and Hezbollah to keep fighting in Gaza and Lebanon appear to have scuttled any hope that the death of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar might hasten an end to the Middle East war.

Economic uncertainty also is a prevailing factor in gold's rise, exacerbated by the upcoming U.S. election, as the policies of both presidential candidates are seen as adding to deficit spending.

Consistently strong U.S. labor market data has reduced hopes for another big Federal Reserve rate cut, but the market is still pricing in smaller rate cuts before year-end - typically a boost for non-interest bearing bullion, as it lowers the opportunity cost.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed +0.8% on Friday to $2,713.70/oz, topping the all-time high set in the previous session, and its 2.1% gain for the week was the sixth consecutive weekly increase.

Also, front-month October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished Friday +4.6% to $33.032/oz, its highest settlement value since December 2012; for the week, silver rose 4.8%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Precious metals mining stocks hitting 52-week highs during Friday's trading: Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM), ASA Gold (ASA), Gatos Silver (GATO), Iamgold (IAG), Kinross Gold (KGC), Newmont (NEM), Osisko Gold Royalties (OR), Pan American Silver (PAAS), Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM).

Private investors are taking profits at current record high levels, but the pace of liquidations is hardly a rush, as investors are not selling as fast as prices rise, BullionVault director research Adrian Ash says.

"The measured pace of net selling overall shows that investors remain confident in gold's long-term outlook," according to Ash.

data.title: Boston Sci gets FDA okay for Farapulse cardiac mapping products
data.description:
• Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) said it has received FDA approval for its Farawave NAV Ablation Catheter for the treatment of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation and Faraview software.
  • The products are used to provide visualization during pulsed field ablation, or PFA, procedures with the company's Farapulse PFA system.
  • The technologies are compatible with Boston Scientific’s existing cardiac mapping technology and its Opal HDx Mapping System. according to a statement.
  • Boston Scientific received FDA clearance for its Farapulse PFA System for the treatment of atrial fibrillation in January.
